# UC-17.1.73 · Cisco WLC + ISE Wireless Authentication Funnel — Association → Auth → DHCP → Posture

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Operations, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Connecting to wifi has 4 steps: see the network, log in, get an IP address, prove you're allowed. We watch where users drop out of this 4-step process to find which step is broken.*

---

## Description

Tracks the wireless-onboarding funnel — associate → authenticate → DHCP → posture. Identifies which phase fails most for a given SSID, AP, or building.

## Value

Wireless onboarding spans many systems. When users complain 'I can't connect to wifi', root cause may be at any phase. The funnel quickly identifies which phase is responsible — letting operations fix the right system.

## Implementation

Schedule every 30 min. Requires WLC and DHCP logs in Splunk.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Block DHCP for a test SSID. UC-17.1.73 shows funnel drop at DHCP phase.

### Negative scenario

Healthy onboarding. Funnel completes; no drop.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=network sourcetype="cisco:wlc:syslog" "associated" 
| eval phase="associated"
| append [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5400) | eval phase="auth"]
| append [search index=network sourcetype="dhcp" "DHCPACK" | eval phase="dhcp"]
| append [search index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=80003 | eval phase="posture"]
| transaction client_mac maxspan=10m
| stats count by phase
| eval funnel_drop=phase
```

## Visualization

Sankey diagram of funnel; per-SSID drop-off heatmap; per-AP phase failure breakdown.

## Known False Positives

- Devices that never request DHCP (static-IP or VPN apps).
- Posture skipped for devices in posture-exception list.

## References

- [Cisco WLC + ISE Integration Best Practices](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
